zh.lifeSummary: 當Lambert I met de Baard graaf van Leuven-Henegouwen於大約0952年出生在比利時布拉班特洛文時，他的父親Régnier III comte de Hainaut34歲，母親Adela de Dagsbourg24歲。他於1003年9月9日與Gerberge de Basse-Lotharingie在義大利托斯卡納費倫結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他於1015年登記服兵役。他去世於1015年9月12日在比利時納姆爾福洛倫斯享年64歲，埋葬在比利時布拉班特 瓦隆尼維勒斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lambert I met de Baard graaf van Leuven-Henegouwen于大约0952年出生在比利时布拉班特洛文时，他的父亲Régnier III comte de Hainaut34岁，母亲Adela de Dagsbourg24岁。他于1003年9月9日与Gerberge de Basse-Lotharingie在意大利托斯卡納費倫结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他于1015年登记服兵役。他去世于1015年9月12日在比利时納姆爾福洛倫斯享年64岁，埋葬在比利时布拉班特 瓦隆尼維勒斯。

nameMeanings.name: graaf
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Egon, Lothar, Otto, Armin, Claus, Erwin, Franz, Friedrich, Hans, Helmut, Hermann, Jochen.
Dutch: variant of Graaf, see De Graaf .

variants.name: Graf
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Kurt, Otto, Erwin, Ernst, Fritz, Gerhard, Hermann, Horst, Manfred, Udo, Alois.

German (also Gräf): status name from Middle High German grāve, grābe, which was used as a title denoting various more or less aristocratic dignitaries and officials. In later times it became established as a title of nobility equivalent to the Romance count. The vocabulary word also denoted a variety of different minor local functionaries in different parts of Germany. In the Grand Duchy of Hesse, for example, it was used for the holder of the comparatively humble office of village headman (compare Mayer , Schulz , and Vogt ). The surname could have originated from any of these senses or be a metonymic occupational or status name for a servant or retainer of a count, or a nickname for someone who gave himself airs and graces. This surname is also found in many other European countries, for example in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Hungary, Poland, Czechia, Slovakia, Croatia, and Slovenia, often as a translation into German of the corresponding Hungarian and Slavic surnames Gróf and Grof . Compare Graef .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name selected, like   Herzog   and other words denoting titles, because of their aristocratic connotations.

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Lambert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, which is from an Old French given name of Germanic origin, from land ‘land, territory’ + beorht ‘famous’. This was introduced to Britain by the Normans, but its frequency in Britain in later centuries owed something to immigrants from the Low Countries, and it continued in occasional use into the 18th century at least. St Lambert of Maastricht was a 7th-century saint who aided St Willibrord in his evangelical work.

variants.name: Lammert
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, which is from an Old French given name of Germanic origin, from land ‘land, territory’ + beorht ‘famous’. This was introduced to Britain by the Normans, but its frequency in Britain in later centuries owed something to immigrants from the Low Countries, and it continued in occasional use into the 18th century at least. St Lambert of Maastricht was a 7th-century saint who aided St Willibrord in his evangelical work.

variants.name: Lamprecht
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, which is from an Old French given name of Germanic origin, from land ‘land, territory’ + beorht ‘famous’. This was introduced to Britain by the Normans, but its frequency in Britain in later centuries owed something to immigrants from the Low Countries, and it continued in occasional use into the 18th century at least. St Lambert of Maastricht was a 7th-century saint who aided St Willibrord in his evangelical work.

nameMeanings.name: met
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Elizabeth
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

nameMeanings.name: Baard
meanings: Irish: Bârd, baird, a poet, or the offspring of a Celtic minstrel

en.lifeSummary: When Lambert I met de Baard graaf van Leuven-Henegouwen was born about 0952, in Leuven, Brabant, Belgium, his father, Régnier III comte de Hainaut, was 34 and his mother, Adela de Dagsbourg, was 24. He married Gerberge de Basse-Lotharingie on 9 September 1003, in Florence, Tuscany, Italy. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. He registered for military service in 1015. He died on 12 September 1015, in Florennes, Namur, Belgium, at the age of 64, and was buried in Nivelles, Walloon Brabant, Belgium.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lambert I met de Baard graaf van Leuven-Henegouwen est né environ 0952, à Leuven, Brabant, Belgique, son père, Régnier III comte de Hainaut, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Adela de Dagsbourg, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Gerberge de Basse-Lotharingie le 9 septembre 1003, à Firenze, Toscana, Italie. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1015. Il est décédé le 12 septembre 1015, à Florennes, Namur, Belgique, à l’âge de 64 ans, et a été enterré à Nivelles, Brabant wallon, Belgique.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lambert I met de Baard graaf van Leuven-Henegouwen um 0952 in Leuven, Brabant, Belgien geboren wurde, war sein Vater Régnier III comte de Hainaut 34 und seine Mutter Adela de Dagsbourg 24. Er hat am 9. September 1003 in Firenze, Toscana, Italien Gerberge de Basse-Lotharingie geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er hat sich 1015 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. Er starb am 12. September 1015 im Alter von 64 Jahren in Florennes, Namen, Belgien und wurde in Nivelles, Wallonisch Brabant, Belgien begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lambert I met de Baard graaf van Leuven-Henegouwen è nato il circa 0952 nel Leuven, Brabante, Belgio, il suo padre, Régnier III comte de Hainaut, aveva 34 anni e il suo madre, Adela de Dagsbourg, aveva 24 anni. Lui sposato Gerberge de Basse-Lotharingie il 9 settembre 1003, nel Firenze, Toscana, Italia. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia. Si è arruolato nell'esercito nel 1015. Morì il 12 settembre 1015, nel Florennes, Namur, Belgio, all'età di 64 anni, e fu sepolto a Nivelles, Brabant wallon, Belgio.

ja.lifeSummary: Lambert I met de Baard graaf van Leuven-Henegouwenが約0952年にベルギーブラバントレウベンで生まれた時，父Régnier III comte de Hainautは34歳，母Adela de Dagsbourgは24歳であった。1003年9月9日にイタリアトスカナフィアーエンズでGerberge de Basse-Lotharingieと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1015年に兵役登録をした。彼は1015年9月12日にベルギーナムルフロアンスで64歳で死去し，ベルギーブラバント ワロンニベレスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Lambert I met de Baard graaf van Leuven-Henegouwen(이)가 대략 0952년 벨기에 브라반트 레우븐에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Régnier III comte de Hainaut(은)는 34세였고, 그의 어머니 Adela de Dagsbourg(은)는 24세였다. 그는 1003년 9월 9일 이탈리아 토스캔아 피렌즈에서 Gerberge de Basse-Lotharingie(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1015년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 그는 1015년 9월 12일 벨기에 남어 플로렌스에서 64세의 나이로 사망했으며 벨기에 브라반트 월런 니벨레스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lambert I met de Baard graaf van Leuven-Henegouwen nasceu aproximadamente 0952, em Leuven, Brabante, Bélgica, seu pai, Régnier III comte de Hainaut, tinha 34 anos e sua mãe, Adela de Dagsbourg, tinha 24 anos. Ele casou-se com Gerberge de Basse-Lotharingie em 9 de setembro de 1003, em Firenze, Toscana, Itália. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 1 filha. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1015. Ele faleceu em 12 de setembro de 1015, em Florennes, Namur, Bélgica, com 64 anos, e foi sepultado em Nivelles, Brabant wallon, Bélgica.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lambert I met de Baard graaf van Leuven-Henegouwen родился около 0952 г. в местности леувен, брабант, Бельгия, его отцу по имени Régnier III comte de Hainaut было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Adela de Dagsbourg, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Gerberge de Basse-Lotharingie 9 сентября 1003 г. в местности фиренз, тоскана, Италия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь. В 1015 году он поступил на военную службу. Он умер 12 сентября 1015 г. в местности флореннесь, намур, Бельгия в возрасте 64 лет и был похоронен в местности нивеллесь, брабант валлон, Бельгия.

sm.from 1003 to 1015: mai le 1003 e o'o I le 1015
sm.after about 0952: ina ua ma'ea e tusa o le 0952

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lambert I met de Baard graaf van Leuven-Henegouwen nació aproximadamente 0952, en Leuven, Brabante, Bélgica, su papá, Régnier III comte de Hainaut, tenía 34 años y su mamá, Adela de Dagsbourg, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Gerberge de Basse-Lotharingie el 9 de septiembre de 1003, en Firenze, Toscana, Italia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 1 hija. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1015. Murió el 12 de septiembre de 1015, en Florennes, Namur, Bélgica, a la edad de 64 años, y fue sepultado en Nivelles, Brabant wallon, Bélgica.
